title: Eliot Wolf refutes report that suggested Tom Brady may have kept Patriots from getting Maxx Crosby
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots executive Eliot Wolf refutes claims that Tom Brady blocked a trade for Raiders' Maxx Crosby.
Crosby trade to Ravens collapsed due to Baltimore's concerns over his medical status, not Brady's influence.
Tom Brady, a minority owner of the Raiders, has no record of interfering in trades involving the Patriots.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 12, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Avra Estiatorio, a Greek restaurant from New York, will open in Boston's Back Bay in mid-April with Aegean cuisine.
Celine, inspired by Montreal's culinary scene, will debut in Seaport in late summer 2026 with artisanal dishes and a robust bar program.
Wonder's Food Halls expand across Greater Boston with six new locations, offering diverse dining options and GrubHub delivery.

title: Baystate Health doctor on probation after lewd behavior inside his office
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Dr. Robert A. Martin received five years probation for masturbating in view of Baystate Health's cancer center.
Martin's medical license suspension was stayed in exchange for probation and participation in behavioral health monitoring.
The Massachusetts Board of Registration in Medicine cited Martin for disruptive behavior and undermining public trust.

title: A brilliant example of biophilic design lists on Martha’s Vineyard for $22,950,000
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Minawetu, a biophilic-designed home on Martha's Vineyard, is listed for $22,950,000.
The 8,200-square-foot home features five en-suite bedrooms, a wine cellar, gym, and a rooftop pool.
Biophilic elements include stone paths, waterfalls, Chelmsford granite, African mahogany, and solar panels for sustainability.

title: Woman injured in Natick during marketplace meet-up to sell $27,000 diamond
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Keyjuan Webb allegedly attempted to steal a 6.1-carat diamond during a meet-up at Natick Mall.
Kathleen Sanfacon was injured while trying to prevent Webb from escaping; a bystander was also bitten.
Webb faces multiple charges, including unarmed robbery and assault, and is held without bail pending a hearing.

title: He’s running Boston to support a family friend’s fight against cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Sean Pruneau, 32, will run the Boston Marathon for Dana-Farber with Team Dennis, honoring Dennis Ford's battle with glioblastoma.
Dennis Ford, a longtime charity organizer, raised over $2.5 million for the Jimmy Fund before his cancer diagnosis in August 2023.
Team Dennis emphasizes "Fueled by Positive Energy" to support Ford and aims to contribute towards a future without cancer.

title: Boston hotels increasing pricing, programming for the World Cup this summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston hotels' prices are surging over 300% for the FIFA World Cup, with The Liberty Hotel charging over $800 per night.
Foxborough's Gillette Stadium, rebranded as Boston Stadium, will host seven World Cup games, expecting over 2 million visitors to Massachusetts.
Boston hotels offer World Cup packages with amenities like luxury suites, dining credits, and game-viewing parties, targeting international tourists.

title: ‘Violent fugitive’ wanted in Lawrence murder arrested in Maine
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jean Michael Warner Delgado, 20, was arrested in Baldwin, Maine for a Lawrence homicide warrant.
Delgado is charged with murder for the September 13 shooting death of Hugo Delrosario in Lawrence.
He awaits extradition to Massachusetts after being taken into custody by the Cumberland County Sheriff’s Office.

title: Jayson Tatum will miss Celtics game against Thunder
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum will miss the Celtics' game against the Thunder due to right Achilles injury management.
Payton Pritchard returns from neck spasms, Derrick White is questionable for the Celtics with a right knee contusion.
The Thunder hold the NBA's best record at 51-15, and the Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference at 43-22.

title: Boston City Council moves toward potential kratom ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council members consider drafting an ordinance to ban kratom, which mimics opioid effects.
Kratom products, including synthetic 7-OH compounds, are raising public health concerns in Boston.
Over 20 Massachusetts cities have banned kratom, with potential statewide legislation being considered.
